channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Work begins to restore power to Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Work has begun to repair the damaged power supply to Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, the head of the U.N.’s nuclear watchdog said Saturday. The repairs are hoped to end a precarious four-week outage that saw it dependent on backup generators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gaza famine claims face mounting scrutiny as mortality data falls far short of predictions
item.description: Experts challenge Gaza famine claims by the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification, citing insufficient mortality data and declining food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 72 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After Zelenskyy meeting, Trump calls on Ukraine and Russia to ‘stop where they are’ and end the war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday called on Kyiv and Moscow to “stop where they are” and end their brutal war following a lengthy White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, SEUNG MIN KIM, MICHELLE L. PRICE and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy pitches Trump on Ukraine drone-for-Tomahawk missile exchange as president weighs escalation concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump shows interest in a potential drone deal with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy but calls Tomahawk missiles a significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Mongolia's prime minister ousted after 4 months in ruling party feud
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Mongolia ’s parliament has voted to oust the prime minister in an unusually public power struggle within the ruling Mongolian People’s Party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen street musician jailed for leading anti-Putin song performance in St. Petersburg: report
item.description: Diana Loginova faces charges for organizing an unauthorized public gathering and discrediting the Russian military after performing an anti-Putin song.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: 'No choice but to go in and kill' Hamas if Gaza bloodshed doesn't stop
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that if Hamas does not heed repeated warnings to stop bloodshed in Gaza, the U.S. will have “no choice” but to use lethal force. “If Hamas continues to kill people in Gaza, which was not the Deal, we will have no choice but to go in and kill them.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Afghanistan’s Taliban government blames Pakistan for twin drone strikes on Kabul
item.description: KABUL, Afghanistan (AP) — Afghanistan’s Taliban government said Thursday that Pakistan had carried out two drone strikes on Kabul the previous day, just before the two neighbors announced a ceasefire following days of fighting that killed dozens in both countries and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: SIDDIQULLAH ALIZAI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Putin plan Hungary meeting on Ukraine war, Zelenskyy to visit White House Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump schedules call with Russian President Vladimir Putin as Ukraine seeks long-range Tomahawk missiles following recent Russian strikes on energy sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department aware of reports after American tourists attacked, 1 killed in popular seaside destination
item.description: State Department aware of reports from coastal town near Lisbon, Portugal, as Cascais police investigate after two Americans attacked, leaving one dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kate Middleton and Prince William set to move into 'forever home': Report
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales, along with their children, have scheduled early November for when they plan to settle into their new Windsor residence.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: US blasts China's expansion of rare earth export controls
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Top U.S. officials on Wednesday blasted China's major expansion of rare earth export controls as a threat to global supply chains, but said Beijing could still change course and avoid steps by Washington to decouple from the world's second-largest economy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Lawder and Andrea Shalal, NewsNation

item.title: IDF says body turned over by Hamas doesn't match any hostages
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced that a corpse surrendered by Hamas was not a match for any of the hostages who are supposed to be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia using new, more precise drone tech to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion in early 2022, railway officials have publicly reported about roughly one attack on railways per week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel frees some Gaza medical staff, but a prominent hospital chief remains imprisoned
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Under Gaza’s ceasefire deal, Israel freed dozens of doctors, nurses, paramedics and other medical personnel seized during raids on hospitals. But more than 100 remain in Israeli prisons, including Dr.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A new form of tourism: US residents seek tattoos in Mexico
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – U.S. residents often cite lower prices and personalized attention when asked why they travel to see doctors and dentists in Mexico. The same holds true for tattoo artists.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Snipers on stadium roof amid heavy security for Italy's win over Israel in World Cup qualifying
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The World Cup qualifier between Italy and Israel took place amid a heavy police presence that included snipers on the stadium roof.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says it has received the remains of 4 more deceased hostages as cuts to aid for Gaza loom
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas hastened Tuesday to ease the pressure on a fragile ceasefire in its war with Israel by returning the bodies of more dead hostages.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Freed hostages' loved ones thank Trump for reuniting them after more than two years
item.description: Several families of the freed Israeli hostages thanked President Donald Trump and his team for brokering the Israel-Hamas deal that brought them home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Therapist explains what Hamas hostages are feeling
item.description: Dr. Rebecca Bailey joins "Banfield" to discuss the mental process the former Hamas hostages will be going through.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's power worldwide has 'increased 1,000%': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Sunday marked 737 days since the hostages were taken, and all 20 hostages remaining alive were released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: How the US brokered the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas saw the release of Israeli hostages and Palestinian prisoners and detainees.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to visit Washington this week seeking long-range weapons and a Trump meeting
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday he will travel to the United States this week for talks on the potential U.S. provision of long-range weapons, a day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Israeli, Palestinian families celebrate return of hostages, prisoners
item.description: The last remaining living Israeli hostages were released as well as more than 1,900 detained Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: From a hilltop village and across Israel: Tears of joy as the living hostages are returned
item.description: LAVON, Israel (AP) — It was a day of joy and tears across Israel. Families, loved ones and friends watched as Hamas released the last living hostages held captive for over two years in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9
